title: Method for recognizing a gesture and an electronic device thereof
abstract: To recognize a gesture and control a function in an electronic device, an operating method of an electronic device includes the operations of detecting a change of a Radio Frequency (RF) signal emitted into a body using an RF sensor, determining a gesture corresponding to the RF signal based on reference data corresponding to the gesture, and executing a function of the electronic device corresponding to the determined gesture.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A method for operating an electronic device, the method comprising: detecting a change of a radio frequency (RF) signal emitted into a body using an RF sensor; determining a gesture corresponding to the RF signal based on reference data for at least one gesture; and executing a function of the electronic device corresponding to the determined gesture. The method of claim 1, further comprising: determining the reference data based on RF signals reflected from the body. The method of claim 2, wherein determining the reference data comprises: sending at least one RF signal to the body while a user of the electronic device conducts a particular gesture; receiving the at least one RF signal reflected from the body; and determining the reference data for the particular gesture based on the at least one RF signal reflected. The method of claim 3, wherein determining the reference data for the particular gesture based on the at least one RF signal reflected comprises: separating the at least one RF signal reflected into a first RF signal representing amplitude and a second RF signal representing phase; converting the first RF signal and the second RF signal to digital signals; and determining the reference data for the particular gesture by processing the digital signals based on artificial neural network (ANN) learning. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting the change of the RF signal using the RF sensor comprises: sending the RF signal to the body; receiving the RF signal reflected from the body; and detecting the change of the RF signal based on comparison of the reflected RF signal and a previously collected RF signal. An electronic device comprising: a radio frequency (RF) sensor; and at least one processor functionally coupled with the RF sensor, wherein the at least one processor detects a change of RF signals emitted into a body using the RF sensor, determines a gesture corresponding to the RF signals based on reference data for at least one gesture, and executes a function of the electronic device corresponding to the determined gesture. The electronic device of claim 6, wherein the at least one processor determines the reference data based on RF signals reflected from the body. The electronic device of claim 7, wherein the RF sensor sends at least one RF signal to the body while a user of the electronic device conducts a particular gesture, and receives the at least one RF signal reflected from the body, and the at least one processor determines the reference data for the particular gesture based on the at least one RF signal reflected. The electronic device of claim 8, wherein the at least one processor separates the at least one RF signal reflected into a first RF signal representing amplitude and a second RF signal representing phase, converts the first RF signal and the second RF signal to digital signals, and determines the reference data for the particular gesture by processing the digital signals based on artificial neural network (ANN) learning. The electronic device of claim 6, wherein the RF sensor sends the RF signal to the user body, receives the RF signal reflected from the body, and detects the change of the RF signal based on comparison of the reflected RF signal and a previously collected RF signal. The electronic device of claim 10, wherein the at least one processor separates the reflected RF signal into a first RF signal representing amplitude and a second RF signal representing phase, converts the first RF signal and the second RF signal to digital signals, and determines the corresponding gesture by processing the digital signals based on ANN learning. The electronic device of claim 6, wherein the at least one processor measures a vibration level of the body using at least one sensor, and determines whether the measured vibration level exceeds a preset threshold. The electronic device of claim 12, wherein the at least one processor measures vibration levels of the body when the user of the electronic device conducts a gesture, selects a maximum vibration level from the measured vibration levels, and determines the maximum vibration level as the threshold. The electronic device of claim 12, wherein, when the measured vibration level exceeds the threshold and an operating frequency of the RF sensor is a high frequency band, the at least one processor switches the operating frequency band of the RF sensor to a low frequency band. The electronic device of claim 12, wherein, when the measured vibration level falls below the threshold, the at least one processor maintains the operating frequency of the RF sensor.
